original_query: M15 Bearish Displacement after H1 Liquidity Sweep

What signals a strong displacement move?

final_chunks.section_title: IDENTIFYING FAIR VALUE GAPS (FVG) AFTER DISPLACEMENT
final_chunks.text:
IDENTIFYING FAIR VALUE GAPS (FVG) AFTER DISPLACEMENT
After a swing high is taken out and an energetic break below it occurs: Look for a clear Fair Value Gap (FVG) formed by a smashing down movement. The low, the high, and if price trades back up into that FVG, you look for a short entry.

E-MINI S&P 15-MINUTE
On the 15-minute Candlestick chart for E-mini S&P: In the morning, prior to 8:30 AM news, a buy side liquidity pool was present.
final_chunks.source: Wednesday May 24, 2023 Forex & Spooz Market Review_Handbook.blocks.json

final_chunks.text:
15-MINUTE CHART
Following the run on the short-term high, the market breaks lower, creating an imbalance. It then trades back up into this imbalance. In this area, you could be a seller and get in sync with the run to get positioned (if you are a swing or short-term trader).

final_chunks.section_title: MARKET STRUCTURE SHIFT AND IMPULSE PRICE LEG IDENTIFICATION
final_chunks.text:
MARKET STRUCTURE SHIFT AND IMPULSE PRICE LEG IDENTIFICATION
On the 15-minute time frame, highlight the market structure shift. Once this high is taken out, the subsequent price movement forms the impulse price leg.

DISPLACEMENT TOWARDS LIQUIDITY
When observing price, look for decisive displacement, not just a drift. Price indicates it is reaching for something, specifically the liquidity resting below old lows.
final_chunks.source: ICT Institutional Price Action： Micro-Market Structure & Time & Price Concepts Part 02_Handbook.blocks.json

final_chunks.section_title: TRANSITION TO 15-MINUTE CHART AND ENTRY CONTEXT
final_chunks.text:
TRANSITION TO 15-MINUTE CHART AND ENTRY CONTEXT
There was a run on Friday's high, followed by a breakdown. The actual fill time for the short was at 10:33 AM, occurring within a specific candle on the chart. The next steps will delve into the details and rationale behind this entry.

IDENTIFYING DISPLACEMENT & FVG HUNT ZONE
Market structure shifts occur on all timeframes. For intraday trading: The high that forms after the run on liquidity is the 'displacement high. ' The low that breaks the prior short-term low is the 'displacement low.

BEARISH MARKET CONDITIONS
For a bearish liquidity-based bias, align with institutional order flow: Monthly, Weekly, and Daily charts are bearish (making lower lows and lower highs, support levels giving Intraday charts (four hours and less) will be correcting and retracing higher.
final_chunks.source: ICT Mentorship Core Content - Month 04 - Orderblocks_Handbook.blocks.json

HOURLY AND 15-MINUTE MARKET STRUCTURE OVERVIEW
On the 4-hour chart, relative highs were observed, indicating liquidity resting above them. The market traded into the daily Bullish Order Block, where a shift in Market Structure was noted, to be Price moved aggressively into the close of the day.

BEARISH BREAKER

final_chunks.text:
BELLWETHER FOR KEY LEVELS
The 15-minute time frame is the 'bellwether time frame'. Here, you look for: Order blocks (though the focus in this mentorship is fair value gaps due to their repetitive and easy-to This time frame helps frame your day.

15-MINUTE CHART
The market then released to the downside. Around 9:00 AM, then specifically at 9:30 AM, the equity markets open. On the 15-minute time frame, at 9:30, the market runs a short-term high.

15-MINUTE CHART
Dropping down inside that range and zooming in towards the high end of it. This is the 15 minute Candlestick chart. So 15 minutes, we can see that the high forms in here around the nine o'clock in the morning hour on Friday. It rallies up, creating what. A Judas swing.

NEW YORK LUNCH
During the lunch hour, if the market is bearish, it will clear stops above highs.

BEARISH ENTRIES
Note the European opening price on Tuesday and filter all shorts at or above this price level. Anticipate a 15-minute chart optimal trade entry to form inside of the retracement higher during London open and/or New York open kill zones, or a buy stop raid.

HIGH PROBABILITY SHORT SETUP
Within the 7:00 AM to 10:00 AM bracket on the 5-minute chart, for a short setup, the primary threshold is to be above the midnight opening price. After hitting the order block, the market drops, then retraces back up ahead of the 10:00 AM news driver.

MORNING MANIPULATION AND AFTERNOON RETRACEMENT
Transitioning to the 15-minute timeframe, we observe Friday the 13th's price action. A 9:30 AM manipulation event took out the overnight London low, creating a setup idea, and then rallied. I was not available to participate in this initial rally.

final_chunks.section_title: PURPOSE OF LIQUIDITY SWEEPS AND THE 'REAL MOVE' TARGET
final_chunks.text:
PURPOSE OF LIQUIDITY SWEEPS AND THE 'REAL MOVE' TARGET
The market takes out these long holders because they are not meant to profit from the 'real move. ' The real move was hinted at 12553. 25, which was the true draw on liquidity.

BEARISH BREAKER AND LIQUIDITY PREFERENCE
When we dropped down to a 60-minute chart, in here we're trading up in here, and we failed to get into that 4-hour old Buy-Side Imbalance Sell-Side Efficiency (BISI) that I wanted to see act as an inversion fair value gap. It never got there.

DXY 15-MINUTE ANALYSIS
On the 15-minute time frame, buy-side liquidity here and sell-side liquidity here during the London session. The midnight opening candle had its opening price extended through. If bearish, looking for lower prices, meaning it is a risk-on scenario.

DXY 15-MINUTE
The Monday New York midnight opening price is extended across the entire week. Price traded up into a fair value gap during the New York open on the 8:30 AM non-farm payroll Friday.

final_chunks.section_title: NEW YORK SESSION STRATEGY FOR EQUITIES AFTER OVERNIGHT RALLIES
final_chunks.text:
NEW YORK SESSION STRATEGY FOR EQUITIES AFTER OVERNIGHT RALLIES
Specific guidance for equities after big overnight moves (not Forex): Wait until after lunch, around 1:00 PM New York time. Anticipate the New York lunch lows or morning session lows to be taken out.

E-MINI S&P 15-MINUTE
On the 15-minute time frame for the E-mini S&P (ES): Reference is made to the 'one setup for life' tutorial for classifying specific liquidity pools, including London session highs/lows and New York PM session buy/sell liquidity.
